supervisor.reason: The new scope is complete: run and resume now use a repo-level .session marker, run starts a fresh session when the marker is missing, omitted --workdir values resolve to the current directory, and focused automated validation passed.

bootstrap.goal: Update dormammu so omitted --cwd work defaults to the current directory, and add a repo-level .session marker that links run and resume to the same session automatically.

tasks.resume_checkpoint: Resume after the .session marker and current-directory workdir slice. Dormammu now writes a repo .session marker, run creates a fresh session when the marker is missing, resume prefers the marker before the root .dev index, omitted --workdir values resolve to the current shell directory, and focused automated tests passed.

items.text: Phase 1. Inspect the CLI session-selection flow and agent workdir forwarding so the new .session marker and current-directory defaults land in the right layer
Phase 2. Change run to create a new session when .session is absent, reuse the marker when it exists, and keep resume aligned with the same session marker behavior
Phase 3. Default external agent workdir resolution to the current directory when --workdir is omitted while preserving explicit overrides
Phase 4. Add focused regression tests for .session creation and reuse, missing-marker session bootstrap, marker-driven resume selection, and current-directory cwd forwarding
Phase 5. Run focused automated validation for the updated CLI and agent adapter behavior

next_action: Prepare a commit for the validated .session marker and current-directory workdir change, or extend the operator docs around the new session marker workflow.
notes: Treat this file as machine truth and keep Markdown synchronized.
DASHBOARD.md should show the actual progress of the active scope.
PLAN.md should list prompt-derived development items in `[ ] Phase N. <title>` format.
The installed guidance bundle and packaged asset copy should stay synchronized.
Legacy TASKS.md snapshots should be migrated forward into PLAN.md without losing checklist state.
This slice targets repo-local session continuation and default current-directory workdir behavior.
The CLI now writes the active session id to a repo-level .session file and reuses it across run and resume.
When .session is missing, run now creates a fresh session instead of silently reusing the root .dev active session.
Omitted --workdir values now resolve to the current shell directory for agent execution and forwarded --cwd flags.
Focused validation passed through `python3 -m unittest tests.test_agent_cli_adapter` and `python3 -m unittest tests.test_cli`.
